column1: WOW!  As we just posted, the NHC has indeed moved the track of what is now Tropical Depression #4 quite a bit to the West. The center has reformed South of Cuba and West of its original spot. This is certainly good news for our area yet it also increases the chance of a stronger storm in the Big Bend Area. Updates to come shortly after the newscast. Check us out at 11 on ABC Action News.
It will change 50 more times between now and tomorrow
is listen to the guy who does this for a living.
correct weather changes
Not to that extreme.
FACTS
Great news for us! We are on Margaritaville at Sea cruise and heading back from Cozumel to Tampa but they have closed Port of Tampa for Sunday so we get an extra day at sea!

now that you are official Floridians, THIS is who you want to follow for the most up to date and accurate storm info, if you’re not already following.

Not to be "that guy" but I have SERIOUS fears for the big bend area that this is going to rapidly intensify and hit much harder than is predicted... now it's forecasted to have almost 36 hrs in the warm gulf waters and if I lived anywhere in that area I'd be preparing for a possible major impact.

I was thinking the same. I was so hopeful when the earlier forecast slammed it into the state down by ft Myers as a weak disorganized rainmaker. Not wishing impacts on anyone, but that would have kept it weak. Now with the west trending it's much more alarming. We ran from Idalia, staying for this one, but feeder bands and tornados will most likely be are biggest threat here in Dunnellon. Weather is like conspiracy theories, worst case happens to be correct a good bit of the time, and when it's not, we breathe easy. My guess is Cat 2-3 on a westward path at current projected speed. I'm just a weather hobbiest, so take that with a grain of salt.

I was honestly hoping for the same thing. Have enough time to skid along the coast that it wouldn't build up or intensify. But it's looking more serious than that unfortunately.. and I know meteorologists work with the info they're currently given and don't want to possibly scare people in areas that may not get ultimately affected by these storms, that's their job and duty to do so. But living in florida my whole life i know that what is predicted days out rarely reflects the actual strength of what they can be, especially given time in the bathwater that is the gulf... stay safe up there and be careful!!

they have a tough battle. Dancing between supposed hype, or underplaying it. I'm not envious of them or their position. I don't expect winds to be too awful, shy of tornado possibilities, but we're in the trees, so windspeed at ground is greatly diminished. No flood threat here either. I truly thank you for the concern. I'm not sure where you're at, but please be safe. I'm still praying she cooks along fast and spares the intensity.

It was predicted to be a cat 1 and I offered for her to come down and stay with me just in case, within 6 hrs it turned from a cat 1 to a cat 3 prediction and became nearly a 5 when impacted. Again, I know the circumstances are different but it's enough time and warmth to become something serious with these forecasts...
